title: Week 01 - Variables
description: Practice sequential calculation, variable updates, variable swapping, and addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and modulo operations.

questions.title: How Much Health Is Left After a Game Character Is Attacked

description.background: A game character enters a level with some initial health. After battle starts, a monster attacks the character once and deals fixed damage. The system needs to update the character's current health after the attack so it can later decide whether the character can continue fighting.
description.task: Calculate the remaining health after the attack from the character's original health and the damage taken this time.
description.input: Two numbers: hp and damage, representing the character's initial health and the damage taken this time.
description.output: Return the character's remaining health after the attack.
description.hint: You can save the updated health in a variable first, then return that variable.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(hp, damage)
    local remainHp = hp - damage
    return remainHp
end

questions.title: How Much Do the Pencils Cost at the Stationery Store

description.background: A stationery store sells one kind of pencil at the same price per pencil. A student plans to buy several pencils and needs to know how much to pay before checkout.
description.task: Calculate the total cost of this pencil purchase from the unit price and quantity.
description.input: Two numbers: price and count, representing the price of each pencil and the quantity purchased.
description.output: Return the total amount to pay for these pencils.
description.hint: You can save the total price in a variable first, then return that variable.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(price, count)
    local total = price * count
    return total
end

questions.title: What Is the Area of the Rectangular Playground

description.background: A rectangular training area has been marked on the school playground. The PE teacher needs to know how large this area is to arrange student groups and equipment placement.
description.task: Calculate the area of the training area from its length and width.
description.input: Two numbers: length and width, representing the length and width of the rectangular area.
description.output: Return the area of this rectangular training area.
description.hint: You can save the calculated area in a variable first, then return the result.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(length, width)
    local area = length * width
    return area
end

questions.title: How Many Coins Are in the Bag After Defeating a Monster

description.background: A game character originally has some coins in the bag. After defeating a monster, the character receives a new coin reward. The system needs to add this reward to the bag and update the character's current coin count.
description.task: Calculate the updated coin total from the original coin count and the reward earned this time.
description.input: Two numbers: coins and reward, representing the original coin count and the newly earned coin reward.
description.output: Return the total number of coins after receiving the reward.
description.hint: You can use a new variable to represent the updated coin count.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(coins, reward)
    local totalCoins = coins + reward
    return totalCoins
end

questions.title: How Much Money Is Left After Buying Pencils and Erasers

description.background: A student brings a fixed amount of money to a stationery store. The student plans to buy several pencils and several erasers. The unit prices and quantities for both items are already known, and the student needs to know how much money will be left after checkout.
description.task: Calculate the cost of the pencils and the cost of the erasers separately, then calculate the remaining amount after shopping.
description.input: Five numbers: money, pencilPrice, pencilCount, eraserPrice, and eraserCount, representing the total money, pencil unit price, pencil quantity, eraser unit price, and eraser quantity.
description.output: Return the amount of money left after buying these stationery items.
description.hint: You can save the costs of the two stationery items separately, then subtract those costs from the total money.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(money, pencilPrice, pencilCount, eraserPrice, eraserCount)
    local pencilTotal = pencilPrice * pencilCount
    local eraserTotal = eraserPrice * eraserCount
    local leftMoney = money - pencilTotal - eraserTotal
    return leftMoney
end

questions.title: How Many Meters Have Been Run After Training

description.background: An athlete records accumulated running distance every day. Before today's training starts, there is already an accumulated distance; after today's training ends, a new running distance is added. The coach needs to update the athlete's accumulated training data.
description.task: Calculate the accumulated running distance after training from the previous accumulated distance and today's added distance.
description.input: Two numbers: beforeMeters and todayMeters, representing the accumulated distance before training and the distance added today.
description.output: Return the accumulated running distance after training.
description.hint: You can save the new accumulated distance in a variable first, then return the result.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(beforeMeters, todayMeters)
    local totalMeters = beforeMeters + todayMeters
    return totalMeters
end

questions.title: Update the Seat Statistics Card After Two Students Swap Seats

description.background: Two students in a classroom temporarily swap seats. The teacher needs to record the swapped seat numbers on a seat statistics card and also calculate the sum, difference, average, and a remainder code for group checking. This record will be used later to verify whether the seat adjustment is correct.
description.task: First swap the two students' seat numbers, then use the swapped numbers to complete several statistics calculations.
description.input: Three numbers: seatA, seatB, and groupSize, representing the two students' original seat numbers and the group size used for checking.
description.output: Return six values: swapped seatA, swapped seatB, the seat number sum, the seat number average, the difference between the two seat numbers, and the group-check remainder.
description.hint: This problem uses addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and modulo. In Lua, the modulo operator is %. Pay attention to operator precedence: multiplication, division, and modulo usually run before addition and subtraction. For complex calculations, split the work into multiple variables.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(seatA, seatB, groupSize)
    local temp = seatA
    seatA = seatB
    seatB = temp
    local total = seatA + seatB
    local average = total / 2
    local gap = seatA - seatB
    local checkCode = seatA * seatB
    local remainder = checkCode % groupSize
    return seatA, seatB, total, average, gap, remainder
end

questions.title: Final Health Settlement After Consecutive Battles

description.background: A game character enters consecutive battles with some health. During battle, the character takes multiple attacks; after battle ends, the character uses several potions to restore health. In addition to basic healing, the potions create extra healing according to a rule. The system also needs to calculate the remainder code used to display the final health by group.
description.task: Follow the battle process in order: handle total damage, potion healing, bonus healing, and final-health grouping, then return the final health and group remainder.
description.input: Four numbers: hp, damage, heal, and potionCount, representing initial health, damage per attack, basic healing per potion, and the number of potions used.
description.output: Return two values: the final health and the group remainder for that final health.
description.hint: Split this problem into steps: process damage first, then healing, and finally modulo. Pay attention to operator precedence, especially multiplication, division, and modulo. Do not squeeze all calculations into one line.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(hp, damage, heal, potionCount)
    local currentHp = hp
    local totalDamage = damage * 2
    currentHp = currentHp - totalDamage
    local totalHeal = heal * potionCount
    local bonusHeal = totalHeal / 2
    currentHp = currentHp + totalHeal + bonusHeal
    local remainder = currentHp % potionCount
    return currentHp, remainder
end

questions.title: Store Bundle Discount, Packaging Fee, and Change Settlement

description.background: A store is selling one kind of bundle, and each bundle has the same price. A student brings some money and buys several bundles. At checkout, the cashier needs to calculate the original total price first, then calculate the promotion discount. The bundles are packed in fixed-size groups, and loose bundles that cannot make a complete group create an extra packaging fee. Finally, the cashier needs to calculate the change from the student's payment.
description.task: Complete the full checkout process: calculate the original total, promotion discount, loose bundle count, packaging fee, discounted payable amount, and change.
description.input: Three numbers: money, packPrice, and packCount, representing the student's payment, bundle unit price, and purchase quantity.
description.output: Return four values: the discounted payable amount, the change, the average discounted amount per bundle, and the loose bundle count.
description.hint: This problem uses addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and modulo. You can handle unit price and quantity multiplication first, then division for the discount, then use % to find the loose bundle count, and finally calculate the payable amount and change. Pay attention to operator precedence.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(money, packPrice, packCount)
    local total = packPrice * packCount
    local discount = total / 10
    local looseCount = packCount % 3
    local packageFee = looseCount * 1
    local finalTotal = total - discount + packageFee
    local change = money - finalTotal
    local average = finalTotal / packCount
    return finalTotal, change, average, looseCount
end
